---
title: "Fishery Management Plan - Assignment Instructions"
format: html
---

## Overview

The purpose of this assignment is for you to develop a management plan for the fishery you chose that will direct activities for the near future. A fishery management plan requires three major tasks:

1. Develop an information base from the data you have and a search of relevant scientific literature.
2. Develop a goal and an accompanying management plan for your fishery.
3. Prepare a written management plan (expanded outline) and report on your management plan in an oral presentation to the class.

Your team will be evaluated as a group, and team members will evaluate the performance of each individual in the group. Your grade will be based on a written expanded outline of your management plan, an oral presentation, and peer evaluations.

## How to Choose a Scenario

Each team will choose one of seven available scenarios. To make your choice:

1. Browse each scenario folder (scenario_01 through scenario_07).
2. Read the `scenario_overview.md` file in each folder to understand the setting, species, management context, and available data.
3. Open the CSV dataset(s) in each folder. Each CSV file contains a data dictionary in commented header rows (lines starting with `#`) that describe every column, its units, and what the values represent.
4. Discuss as a team which scenario is the best match for your interests and strengths.
5. Once your team selects a scenario, notify the instructor so no two teams work on the same one (unless otherwise permitted).

## What You Must Decide

Your team is responsible for developing your own management goal, objectives, and actions. The scenario folders provide data and context, not answers. Specifically, your team must:

- Propose a long-range management goal appropriate to the fishery and its stakeholders.
- Diagnose problems and potentials based on your analysis of the data and your literature search.
- Set quantifiable objectives with dates for accomplishment.
- Prescribe management actions that address fish, habitat, and people.
- Design an implementation timeline and an evaluation plan.

No "correct" goal or set of objectives is provided. Different teams working on the same scenario could reasonably propose different management directions.

## Required Components of the Management Plan Expanded Outline

Your expanded outline must follow the eight steps of the management process listed below. Each step should be developed with enough detail that a reader can evaluate your plan without attending the oral presentation.

### Step 1. Management Goal

- State a long-range vision of the future based on the goal of your management.
- Articulate a clear view of the future that anyone can understand.

### Step 2. Analysis of Resources

- Physical attributes, such as surface area, watershed area, littoral area, thermal stratification, and other key features. These may or may not be available in your data; you may need to do an online search.
- Chemical attributes, such as alkalinity, hardness, dissolved oxygen, and pH. These may or may not be available in your data; you may need to do an online search.
- Biological attributes, such as fish community, plant community, and non-native species. These may or may not be available in your data; you may need to do an online search.
- Human attributes, such as angling pressure, angling regulations, land use in the watershed, and shoreline development. These may or may not be available in your data; you may need to do an online search.

### Step 3. Diagnosis of Problems and Potentials

- Diagnose relevant problems with the habitat, fish, or human users.
- Identify the resource potential that might overcome each problem.

### Step 4. Management Objectives and Actions

- Set quantifiable objectives with dates for accomplishment for each.
- Each objective should relate to a resource problem identified in the previous step.
- Prescribe one or more management actions to achieve each objective.
- Management actions related to fish, habitat, and people (for each objective) are more likely to succeed than management actions related only to fish, habitat, or people.

### Step 5. Organization of People, Equipment, and Budgets

- Describe needs for human effort (time = money).
- Describe needs for equipment (purchases and operation = money).
- Describe needs for funding (supplies and travel = money).

### Step 6. Implementation Plan

- Design implementation so that effects of each management action can be determined.
- Prescribe a timeline for implementing the management plan.
- Indicate when each management action will be implemented.

### Step 7. Evaluation Plan

- Describe how achievement of management objectives will be evaluated.
- Describe interim evaluations that may be needed to evaluate progress.

### Step 8. Maintenance Plan

- Describe how the management plan will be maintained.
- Describe how the management plan will be updated.

## Information Search and Use of Scientific Literature

The data you obtained on your fishery should be analyzed to provide an information base for your decision-making process. You should also use library resources to locate information on similar fisheries. Some information may be available in technical journals, such as:

- Transactions of the American Fisheries Society
- North American Journal of Fisheries Management
- Canadian Journal of Fisheries and Aquatic Sciences

Cite all references, tables, and figures in the text of your plan. Format citations according to the North American Journal of Fisheries Management Guide for Authors.

## Data Analysis and Interpretation

Your team should use the datasets provided in your scenario folder to build the information base for your plan. Depending on the scenario, you should aim to:

- Estimate population parameters such as catch per unit effort (CPUE), relative abundance, growth rates, and mortality rates.
- Compare metrics across years, seasons, sites, or species as appropriate.
- Summarize community composition using indices of diversity, species richness, or proportional abundance.
- Characterize size structure using length-frequency distributions, proportional size distribution (PSD), or related metrics.
- Summarize environmental conditions and identify trends, seasonal patterns, or potential habitat limitations.
- Summarize harvest, angling effort, and angler satisfaction if creel data are available.
- Visualize patterns and relationships using clear figures (scatter plots, bar charts, box plots, time series plots).

Not every analysis listed above will apply to every scenario. Choose the analyses that are relevant to your fishery and the data you have.

## Expectations for Tables and Figures

- Include tables and figures that support your analysis and your plan.
- Each table and figure must be cited in the text of the expanded outline.
- Tables and figures should be numbered sequentially (Table 1, Table 2, ...; Figure 1, Figure 2, ...).
- Provide informative titles and axis labels.
- Format references, tables, and figures according to the North American Journal of Fisheries Management Guide for Authors.

## Presentation Requirements

- Prepare an oral presentation based on the outline of your management plan.
- The oral report should include all major elements of the written plan, but may also include materials such as photos that are not included in the written plan.
- All team members must participate in preparation of the oral report and in its delivery.
- PowerPoint presentations are required.
- The presentation should be no longer than 15 minutes.
- Audience questions should be no longer than 5 minutes (total time = 20 minutes).
- Attendance is mandatory for all presentations.

## Evaluation Rubric Summary

Your written plan and presentation will be evaluated using the scoring breakdown below.

### Expanded Outline (100 points)

| Component                          | Points |
|------------------------------------|--------|
| Goal                               | 10     |
| Analysis of Resource (if possible) | 10     |
| Diagnosis of Problems & Potentials | 10     |
| Management Program                 | 25     |
| Organization of Program            | 10     |
| Implementation of Program          | 5      |
| Evaluate and Maintain Program      | 5      |
| References, Tables, & Figures      | 10     |
| Style & Clarity                    | 10     |
| Evaluation from group              | 10     |
| **TOTAL**                          | **100**|

### Presentation (50 points)

| Component              | Points |
|------------------------|--------|
| Presenter appearance   | 5      |
| Delivery               | 5      |
| Organization           | 5      |
| Visuals                | 10     |
| Substance              | 20     |
| Questions              | 5      |
| Evaluation from group  | 5 (extra credit) |
| **TOTAL**              | **50** |

### Grand Total: 150 points (+5 extra credit)

Team-member evaluations are due on the date of your oral presentation. Evaluations are confidential and will never be disclosed to anyone else.

## Due Dates

- **Expanded Outline of Management Plan:** Thursday, May 1st
- **Oral Presentations:** May 6th

Late plans will not be accepted.
